measurement_or_description: Weather Bureau station survey documented the transported smoke pall across a broad U.S. region

location_scope: Texas and neighboring EPA Region 6 states
measurement_or_description: EPA documented the transboundary haze and coordinated monitoring and health information
EPA annual trends report mapped the smoke and dust episode and discussed U.S. air-quality effects
EPA reported 24-hour PM2.5 of 55.7 µg/m³ in Dallas; 49.6 and 43.7 in San Antonio; 41.3 in Brownsville; and 34.5 in Galveston
EPA published its second monitoring update for the same attributed smoke episode
NOAA GOES imagery through NASA showed smoke plumes from fires in many western states
SeaWiFS image showed a thick smoke pall over both states
MODIS documented dense valley smoke and NASA reported health advisories
SeaWiFS documented smoke across the Bering Strait into Alaska with probable later haze near Washington
NASA documented smoke from Mexico and Central America drifting over the U.S. Gulf Coast
MODIS documented Quebec fire smoke crossing the United States to the Atlantic
NASA documented transported smoke and elevated surface ozone
NASA collection documented repeated smoke columns from major Oregon fires
MODIS documented widespread smoke that hampered aerial fire operations
SeaWiFS documented smoke streamers reaching southern Florida
MODIS documented large smoke columns from the Booth and Bear Butte fires
MISR documented plume extent and height from multiple fires
NASA MOPITT imagery documented carbon monoxide from the fires
TOMS documented high aerosol concentrations and eastward transport
MODIS documented a thick smoke blanket over eastern Alaska
NASA documented transported particle pollution reaching Texas and described the NASA-EPA-NOAA forecast system
NASA summarized analysis estimating increased ground-level ozone from 2004 boreal fires
MODIS documented statewide smoke and cited Alaska air-quality warnings
NPS smoke-condition archive documents dense regional smoke during the 1988 fires
NPS incident history corroborates the source-fire period and geography
EPA trends reporting documents monitored pollution effects and public alerts attributed to the wildfire outbreak
MODIS documented smoke from numerous fires across the central and southern Appalachians
